title: Secret setup guided fixes and the all-8-field settings editor
action: Two additions to doctor-config-surface-4's phase-2 fix offer and a new end-of-run settings editor. First, extend phase 2 (interactive-only, still gated by prompt::is_interactive(), --check still skips this entirely) with a guided fix for the 'web token' check (checks.rs, uses ensure_web_secret_readonly_impl at checks.rs:242-259 for its read-only diagnosis - that helper only DIAGNOSES and creates nothing) when it is failing for a fixable reason: confirm, then call the real creation/repair path, src/config/mod.rs's existing `ensure_web_secret()` (mod.rs:775, returns the secret and whether it was newly created) - do not call the read-only checks.rs helper expecting it to fix anything, and do not reimplement token creation. For github/telegram tokens, which have no dedicated diagnostic check today, do not add new checks in this cell - their setup lives only in the settings editor below, per CONTEXT.md's Terms ('secrets' are part of the config surface, not new diagnostic checks). Second, add src/doctor/edit.rs, declared as a new `mod edit;` inside src/doctor/mod.rs (the doctor submodule pattern used by `mod checks;`/`pub mod prompt;` at mod.rs:4-5 - src/lib.rs only has `pub mod doctor;` at lib.rs:10 and is not touched by this cell): a settings editor reached through exactly one end-of-run prompt after phase 3's recheck report - 'edit a setting? [y/N]' (default no, per D17 - no --edit flag, no subcommand). On yes, walk all 8 config.json fields (bind_addr, herdr_session, allowed_roots, poll_interval_ms, herdr_protocol, static_dir, herdr_socket, telegram_chat_id - src/config/mod.rs's Config/write::CONFIG_FIELDS order) plus the 3 env-only secrets (web/github/telegram, via src/config/secrets.rs's write_secret and src/doctor/prompt.rs's prompt_secret for masked entry) in a menu (prompt::choose) letting the operator pick which one to edit, edit it, then return to the menu until they choose to stop. Every config.json field edit goes through write::persist_validated (never a raw write) so an invalid candidate is rejected and the existing file is untouched; allowed_roots edits reuse the exact same breadth-classification + typed-confirmation function doctor-config-surface-4 wrote for its guided fix (that cell is expected to factor it into a shared function in checks.rs or write.rs - call that same function here, do not write a second implementation). D16: main.rs already has the non-loopback security warning inline at main.rs:154-166 (`if !config.bind_addr.ip().is_loopback() { ... println!(...) }`) and the loopback check pattern also appears in checks.rs:194 (`c.bind_addr.ip().is_loopback()`) - the moment bind_addr is changed to a non-loopback address in the editor, print the same warning message (or a shared helper extracted from main.rs's text) before the change is persisted; extracting a shared function that both main.rs's startup path and this editor call is preferred over duplicating the message text, but is not required if duplication is simpler - either way the warning must fire at edit time, not only at next startup.

truths: The web-token check's guided fix calls config::ensure_web_secret() (the real creation/repair path), never the read-only checks.rs diagnostic helper.
No new diagnostic check is added for github_token or telegram_bot_token - they are reachable only through the settings editor.

The settings editor is reached by exactly one end-of-run yes/no prompt, defaulting to no, with no --edit flag or subcommand anywhere, and the doctor submodule is declared in src/doctor/mod.rs, not src/lib.rs.
All 8 config.json fields and all 3 secrets are editable from the settings editor menu.
Every config.json field edit through the editor is validated via write::persist_validated before being written; an invalid candidate leaves the existing file untouched.

An allowed_roots addition through the editor calls the exact same breadth-classification + typed-confirmation function doctor-config-surface-4 introduced for its guided fix - not a second implementation.
Changing bind_addr to a non-loopback address in the editor prints the same non-loopback security warning main.rs prints at startup, at edit time before the write happens, not only at next startup.
Secret entry in the editor uses prompt::prompt_secret (masked, non-revealing display) and src/config/secrets.rs's write_secret - never a plaintext prompt, never a hand-rolled env-file writer.
--check and non-interactive invocation never reach the settings editor prompt.
migrate_default_state_if is still never invoked anywhere on the doctor code path, per D12.

artifacts.substantive: end-of-run settings editor covering all 8 config fields plus 3 secrets, calling doctor-config-surface-4's shared allowed_roots breadth-confirmation function rather than reimplementing it
declares mod edit; single end-of-run edit prompt wired after the phase-3 recheck report, --check/non-interactive skip it entirely

key_links: src/doctor/edit.rs calls src/config/write.rs's persist_validated and src/config/secrets.rs's write_secret - it does not reimplement validation or file writing
src/doctor/mod.rs declares `mod edit;` alongside its existing `mod checks;`/`pub mod prompt;` - src/lib.rs is unchanged
the web-token guided fix calls src/config/mod.rs's ensure_web_secret(), not checks.rs's read-only ensure_web_secret_readonly_impl
prohibitions: Do not add an --edit flag, a --settings flag, or any subcommand dispatch, per D17 and decision a4621b71.
Do not add new diagnostic checks for github_token or telegram_bot_token.
Do not write a second allowed_roots breadth-classification/typed-confirmation implementation - call doctor-config-surface-4's shared one.
Do not let the editor reach past --check or non-interactive mode under any flag combination.
Do not print, log, or persist a secret value in plaintext anywhere.
Do not call checks.rs's ensure_web_secret_readonly_impl expecting it to create or repair anything - it is read-only.

verify: cargo test --quiet && cargo clippy --quiet -- -D warnings && bash tests/rename_contract.sh

trace.outcome: Added the web-token guided fix (phase 2) and the end-of-run all-8-field + 3-secret settings editor (new src/doctor/edit.rs). Web-token fix calls config::ensure_web_secret() (real creation path), never the read-only helper, and never echoes the value. Editor is reached only via one end-of-run 'edit a setting? [y/N]' prompt (default no) in interactive mode; --check/non-interactive never reach it. Config edits route through write::persist_validated (invalid candidate leaves file untouched); allowed_roots reuses cell-4's checks::prompt_new_allowed_root breadth guard; bind_addr->non-loopback fires the D16 warning at edit time via a shared herdr_go::doctor::non_loopback_bind_warning helper also called by main.rs startup; secrets use the masked prompt::prompt_secret + secrets::write_secret. No new github/telegram diagnostic checks; no --edit flag/subcommand; migrate_default_state_if untouched (D12).

After implementing real bodies the same command reported 'test result: ok. 35 passed; 0 failed'.",

}
trace.verify_output: cargo test: 152 lib tests passed (incl. 35 doctor:: tests, 12 new for this cell); integration suites 2/2, 3/3, 0/0 passed. cargo clippy --quiet -- -D warnings: clean (chain continued, no warnings). rename_contract.sh: 'rename contract: ok' (exit 0). The 3 'rg: command not found' lines on L30/37/38 are the known sandbox rg-absence noted at dispatch; they sit inside 'if rg ...; then fail' guards so a missing rg is a false condition, not a failure, and the script still exits 0 — identical to the green baseline.
trace.verify_command: cargo test --quiet && cargo clippy --quiet -- -D warnings && bash tests/rename_contract.sh

checks.evidence: checks.rs offer_web_token_fix passes config::ensure_web_secret as the create fn; ensure_web_secret is the real path at src/config/mod.rs:779; doc comment says never the read-only ensure_web_secret_readonly_impl
checks.rs diff adds only offer_web_token_fix + a web token arm in offer_fixes; build_checks not touched; no github/telegram diagnostic Check added
edit.rs maybe_edit calls prompt::confirm(...,"edit a setting?",false) once; mod edit; declared at src/doctor/mod.rs:6, lib.rs untouched; no --edit/--settings/subcommand anywhere in main.rs
edit.rs run_editor builds menu from write::CONFIG_FIELDS (8 fields) plus 3 secrets; test the_menu_exposes_all_eight_fields_and_three_secrets asserts each
edit.rs persist_field -> checks::persist_and_report -> write::persist_validated which runs Config::load_str before write_atomic; test an_invalid_field_value_leaves_the_file_untouched confirms
edit.rs:139 calls checks::prompt_new_allowed_root, single definition at checks.rs:608, also called by cell-4's guided fixes - no second implementation
edit.rs edit_config_field calls the shared super::non_loopback_bind_warning before persist_field; same fn main.rs now calls at startup; test confirms warning fires at edit time
edit.rs edit_secret uses prompt::prompt_secret and config::secrets::write_secret, routing through prepare_token_directory/write_new_token/validate_token_protection
mod.rs run() returns before phase-4 edit::maybe_edit when check_only or !is_interactive()
grep over the 4 touched files hits only pre-existing main.rs lines, none added/altered by this diff
web-token fix discards the returned secret value, reports only outcome; edit.rs prints only entry.display() (D13 non-revealing); test asserts the value never appears in output
